weather.summary: Saturday: 11°C, broken clouds. Sunday: 10°C, light rain

metadata.greeting: This weekend is packed with exciting activities for your little one! From a Candlelight Concert for Babies to family-friendly festivals and museum adventures, there's something for everyone. Don't miss the chance to explore the outdoors with city farms and playgrounds, or get creative with hands-on workshops and performances!

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: A beautifully designed wooden playground with a sweet pond theme, perfect for toddlers.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a safe and engaging space for toddlers to explore and play, with a gentle theme that sparks imagination.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Stratford, then a short walk to the park.
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to the Westfield shopping centre for lunch and baby facilities.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: Two floors of immersive play space known as the Story Worlds as well as the Story Garden playground. Baby Storytelling plus crafts and singalong time for babies and toddlers are often hosted.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a creative and immersive experience for young children, with dedicated spaces for babies and toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Stratford.
transport.tip: Check the website for specific event timings and booking.

activities.title: Library Club
activities.description: A reading corner and diverse book-pairing service for children of all ages.
activities.whyItWorks: Provides a relaxed and engaging environment for children to explore books and develop a love for reading.

transport.fromHackney: Various bus routes available.
transport.tip: Check term times before you go.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: A small city farm with animals, gardens and a cafe.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a chance to see farm animals up close and learn about where food comes from.

transport.fromHackney: Various bus routes available.
transport.tip: Check the website for special events.

activities.title: Museum of the Home
activities.description: Explore homes through the ages, with gardens and a cafe.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a glimpse into the past and how homes have evolved, sparking curiosity about history.

transport.fromHackney: Various bus routes or Overground to Hoxton.
transport.tip: Check for family trails and activities on the website.

activities.title: Spitalfields City Farm
activities.description: A community city farm with animals, gardens and a cafe.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a chance to see farm animals up close and learn about where food comes from.

transport.fromHackney: Various bus routes available.
transport.tip: Check for seasonal events and activities.

activities.title: Stepney City Farm
activities.description: A working city farm with a variety of animals, gardens and a cafe.
activities.whyItWorks: Provides an educational and engaging experience for children to learn about animals and farming.

transport.fromHackney: Various bus routes available.
transport.tip: Check the website for special events and workshops.

activities.title: Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park
activities.description: Explore the vast parklands, playgrounds and water features of the Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a variety of outdoor activities and play areas for children of all ages.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Stratford.
transport.tip: Pack a picnic and enjoy the open spaces.

activities.title: The Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore the wonders of the natural world, from dinosaurs to insects.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a fascinating and educational experience for children of all ages, with interactive exhibits and impressive displays.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Visit early or late to avoid the crowds.

activities.title: The Science Museum
activities.description: Discover the wonders of science and technology, with interactive exhibits and fascinating displays.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a hands-on and engaging experience for children, with plenty to explore and discover.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Check the website for special events and workshops.

activities.title: The British Museum
activities.description: Explore world cultures and history, with iconic artifacts and fascinating exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a cultural and educational experience for children, with iconic artifacts and fascinating stories.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus to Holborn or Russell Square.
transport.tip: Pick up a family trail at the information desk to guide your visit.

activities.title: V&A Museum of Childhood
activities.description: Discover the world of childhood through toys, games and objects from the past.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a nostalgic and engaging experience for children, with plenty to see and do.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus to Bethnal Green.
transport.tip: Check the website for family events and activities.

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.description: Explore natural history, anthropology and musical instruments, with gardens and a butterfly house.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a diverse and engaging experience for children, with something for everyone to enjoy.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Forest Hill.
transport.tip: Visit the animal walk and enjoy the views over London.

activities.title: Cutty Sark
activities.description: Climb aboard this historic clipper ship and discover what life was like at sea.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a hands-on and immersive experience for children to learn about maritime history.

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR to Cutty Sark.
transport.tip: Check for family events and activities on the website.

activities.title: National Maritime Museum
activities.description: Explore maritime history and culture, with interactive exhibits and fascinating displays.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a fun and educational experience for children, with plenty to see and do.

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR to Cutty Sark.
transport.tip: Visit the All Hands gallery for interactive activities for young children.

activities.title: Coram's Fields
activities.description: Seven acres dedicated entirely to children, with an adventure playground, sand pits, a paddling pool and a city farm.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a safe and engaging space for children to play and explore, with a variety of activities to choose from.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus to Russell Square.
transport.tip: Remember that adults are only allowed entry when accompanied by a child.

activities.title: Drop-in Design: Wiggly Weaving
activities.description: A hands-on design workshop where families can create wiggly weaving.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a creative and engaging activity for children to express their artistic skills.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Check the V&A website for specific timings.

activities.title: Pop-Up Performance: Build a Story
activities.description: A fun and interactive performance where families can help build a story.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a creative and engaging experience for children to participate in a live performance.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Check the V&A website for specific timings.

activities.title: Family Activity: Micro to Macro!
activities.description: Explore the world from the micro to the macro with this family activity.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a hands-on and educational experience for children to learn about different scales of the world.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus to Bethnal Green.
transport.tip: Check the V&A website for specific timings.

activities.title: Easter Family Activities - Moving Pictures
activities.description: Create your own spinning animation featuring Tower Bridge, inspired by Victorian phenakistoscopes.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on STEAM activity that introduces animation principles in a fun, engaging way.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus to Tower Hill or London Bridge.
transport.tip: Drop-in, but arrive early to avoid queues.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt
activities.description: Roam around the gardens and find hidden Lindt Gold Bunny statues to win a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor exploration with a sweet reward at the end.

transport.fromHackney: Train to Waterloo, then train to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, especially for weekends.

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt
activities.description: Wander past giraffes, lions, monkeys and more incredible wildlife as you search for hidden eggs on a special Easter egg hunt.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines animal encounters with a fun Easter-themed activity.

transport.fromHackney: Bus to Regent's Park.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance.

activities.title: Easter Egg Trail
activities.description: A self-guided trail through the historic palace interiors and gardens.
activities.whyItWorks: Explore a historic site with a fun, engaging activity.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Putney Bridge, then a short walk.
transport.tip: No advance booking required, purchase on arrival.

activities.title: REPLAY: A Limitless Recycled Playground
activities.description: A play space made out of repurposed materials.
activities.whyItWorks: Encourages creativity and imaginative play with recycled materials.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Waterloo or Embankment.
transport.tip: Check the Southbank Centre website for specific timings and booking.
